cards.title: Epstein suicide note released by May 8?
cards.headline: Epstein Suicide Note Was Released Before May 8 Deadline
cards.summary: A federal judge unsealed a purported Epstein suicide note on May 6-7, 2026, with wall-to-wall coverage from major outlets including NYT, AP, BBC, and CNN — meeting the 'credibly reported' threshold well before the May 8 deadline.
why: Federal judge publicly released the note 1-2 days before the deadline
Every major news outlet (NYT, AP, BBC, CNN, NPR, Time, Axios) reported it as a suicide/goodbye note
Resolution criteria require only 'credibly reported' release — not full authentication
cards.watch_item: The market's near-zero YES price despite overwhelming evidence suggests a possible resolution interpretation issue — watch whether operators define 'suicide note' as requiring connection to Epstein's actual August 2019 death rather than his earlier July attempt.

cards.title: Which countries will report an Andes virus case in May 2026?
cards.headline: US Andes Virus Case Already Confirmed — Market Severely Underpricing at 34.5%
cards.summary: A US citizen from the MV Hondius cruise ship outbreak has been confirmed PCR-positive by CDC and is in Nebraska biocontainment, yet the market prices a US case report in May 2026 at only 34.5% versus a fair value near 88%.
why: CDC confirmed a PCR-positive American citizen, widely reported by Reuters and Forbes
Patient is already in a US biocontainment facility — official reporting is near-certain
Market appears to be dramatically lagging publicly available confirmation news
cards.watch_item: Any reclassification of the 'mildly positive' PCR result as inconclusive, or unusual resolution criteria requiring a specific reporting format not yet published

cards.title: Who will be the next new Prime Minister of Romania?
cards.headline: Eugen Tomac at 46.5% for Romania PM Is a Broken Price — Sell It
cards.summary: Kalshi's Romania PM market is severely dysfunctional, with three candidates each priced at 46.5% (summing to an impossible 139.5%). Eugen Tomac, a minor-party leader with roughly 4% real odds, represents the most egregious mispricing and the clearest sell opportunity.
why: Three candidates at identical 46.5% is mathematically impossible — a clear pricing artifact or illiquidity issue
Tomac leads a minor party with no realistic path to assembling a parliamentary majority
Cross-venue consensus places technocrats like Matei (~22%) and Burnete (~17%) as actual frontrunners, not Tomac
cards.watch_item: Whether the displayed 46.5% price is actually executable — market illiquidity may prevent trades from filling at this level

cards.title: Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz? (5/4 - 5/10)
cards.headline: Strait of Hormuz Traffic Far Below Market Expectations Amid Iran-US Conflict
cards.summary: With only 2-7 ships transiting daily versus the pre-conflict norm of 100+, the market is dramatically overpricing the likelihood of weekly traffic exceeding 40 vessels during May 4-10.
why: Actual daily transits are 2-7 ships due to ongoing attacks, insurance restrictions, and shipowner wariness — far below the ~6/day needed to hit 40
Iran's May 6 safe passage announcement has not meaningfully restored traffic, and new US strikes on May 8 reinforce the hostile environment
Only a few days remain in the measurement window, leaving almost no time for a meaningful traffic surge
cards.watch_item: A sudden ceasefire or diplomatic breakthrough that triggers a rush of queued vessels through the strait before May 10

cards.title: Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz? (5/11 - 5/17)
cards.headline: Strait of Hormuz Traffic Likely to Exceed 20 Ships Despite Ongoing Disruptions
cards.summary: Recent transit data showing 21 vessels in a single reporting period suggests cumulative weekly traffic (May 11-17) will clear the 20-ship threshold, even as US blockade enforcement and collapsed Iran peace talks weigh on volumes.
why: Recent observations confirm ~10-15 ships per day currently transiting, making 20+ over a full week highly probable
Market pricing at 57% significantly undervalues the likelihood given observed vessel flow data
Iran's vetting process is allowing a trickle of compliant vessels through, maintaining baseline traffic
cards.watch_item: Whether the prior week's surprising resolution below 20 ships repeats — a sudden escalation or full blockade enforcement could halt the current trickle of traffic

cards.title: Will Trump take back the Panama Canal?
cards.headline: Panama Canal 'Takeover' Market Overpriced at 32%
cards.summary: Despite Trump's persistent rhetoric about reclaiming the Panama Canal, the structural barriers to actual US government control remain enormous, making the market significantly overpriced relative to a fair probability around 12%.
why: Panama firmly refuses any sovereignty transfer, invoking the 1977 treaty and international law
Resolution requires US government control—private US companies buying port stakes wouldn't count
No historical precedent for forcible seizure of sovereign territory in the modern era, and Congress shows no appetite for it
cards.watch_item: Any negotiated 'security agreement' giving the US an official operational role at the canal could blur the resolution line

cards.title: What will Trump say during the bilateral with China?
cards.headline: Trump Almost Certainly Says 'Tariff' During China Bilateral — Market Underpricing It
cards.summary: With tariffs confirmed as a central agenda item at the Trump-Xi summit in Beijing and Trump's well-documented habit of using the word compulsively, the market at 52.5% significantly underprices the ~72% fair probability he says 'tariff' during the bilateral meeting.
why: Trade/tariffs are explicitly central to the summit agenda per all major outlets
'Tariff' is Trump's signature trade vocabulary — he uses it reflexively in any commerce discussion
Market resolution covers the full bilateral meeting (inclusive of greeting), giving multiple opportunities for the word to appear
cards.watch_item: Whether bilateral remarks are open to press or conducted behind closed doors, which could affect resolution if no public transcript is available

cards.title: When will traffic at the Strait of Hormuz return to normal?
cards.headline: Strait of Hormuz Recovery Before July 2026 Is Heavily Overpriced
cards.summary: With traffic at just 11-21 ships per day versus the 60+ needed for normalization, no ceasefire deal in sight, and the Pentagon estimating 6 months for mine clearing alone, a return to normal before July 1, 2026 is extremely unlikely—yet the market prices it at ~25%.
why: Current traffic is roughly one-third of the threshold needed to declare normalization, with no diplomatic deal signed
Pentagon estimates 6 months of mine clearing after hostilities end—making a 50-day resolution near-impossible
Insurance premiums have surged 20x, creating structural lags that persist even after physical conditions improve
cards.watch_item: Any sudden US-Iran diplomatic breakthrough or unilateral military corridor operation could rapidly shift the timeline

cards.title: Starmer out by May 19, 2026?
cards.headline: Starmer Leadership Crisis Underpriced at 5.5% — Fair Value Near 18%
cards.summary: With a cabinet-level resignation, ~90 Labour MPs calling for his departure, and historical precedent showing PMs can fall within days once the dam breaks, the market significantly underprices Starmer's exit by May 19 despite his public defiance and the tight 4-day window.
why: Health Secretary Streeting's scathing resignation mirrors the Johnson/Truss endgame pattern where collapse followed within days
~90 Labour MPs publicly calling for resignation approaches the critical mass that historically forces departures
Market only requires an announcement of resignation — not the effective date — lowering the bar for resolution
cards.watch_item: Additional cabinet resignations in the next 48 hours — if more senior ministers follow Streeting, Starmer's position becomes untenable regardless of his stated defiance

cards.title: Will Trump recognize Taiwan?
cards.headline: Trump Recognition of Taiwan Remains Extremely Unlikely Despite Market Premium
cards.summary: At 18.5%, the market vastly overprices the chance Trump formally recognizes Taiwan before January 2029. His recent diplomacy with China, zero historical precedent since 1979, and catastrophic geopolitical consequences all point to a fair probability near 7%.
why: Trump warned Taiwan against independence during his May 2026 China visit, treating it as a bargaining chip rather than an ally to elevate
No U.S. president has recognized Taiwan since the One China policy was adopted in 1979—a 46-year base rate of zero
Formal recognition would risk military conflict with China and severe economic fallout, deterring even the most unconventional leader
cards.watch_item: A dramatic breakdown in U.S.-China relations—such as a Chinese military move on Taiwan—could force an emergency recognition scenario that currently isn't priced into the fundamentals

cards.title: Will the US acquire any new territory?
cards.headline: US Territory Acquisition Overpriced — Sell the Hype
cards.summary: The market prices a 35.5% chance the US acquires new territory before January 2029, but current negotiations focus on military bases rather than sovereignty, and key counterparties explicitly reject territorial transfer—putting fair value closer to 24%.
why: US-Denmark/Greenland talks center on military base access, not sovereignty transfer—Greenland's leader calls sovereignty 'non-negotiable'
Venezuela '51st state' rhetoric has zero legal mechanism or pathway to actual acquisition
Denmark's incoming right-leaning government is less likely, not more, to cede Greenland
cards.watch_item: Any shift in resolution criteria interpretation—if 'gains control' could include new military base sovereignty designations (Guantanamo-style), the probability rises significantly

cards.title: How low will Trump's approval rating get before 2027?
cards.headline: Trump's Approval Likely to Dip Below 36% Before 2027
cards.summary: With Trump's approval aggregate already at 38% and individual polls registering as low as 34-35%, a further 2-point drop in the aggregate over the next 7.5 months appears more likely than the market reflects, especially given ongoing pressures from the Iran conflict and economic concerns.
why: Approval aggregate already at 38% with a clear downward trend driven by war and economic anxiety
Individual polls (Reuters/Ipsos) already reading 35%, suggesting the aggregate will follow
7.5 months remain for any single reading to breach the threshold—time favors further decline
cards.watch_item: A ceasefire or diplomatic deal with Iran could stabilize or reverse the approval slide before aggregates hit 36%

cards.title: Who will be the next Prime Minister of the UK?
cards.headline: Wes Streeting Significantly Underpriced as Next UK PM
cards.summary: Streeting is a declared leadership challenger already sitting in Parliament, priced at just 6.6% despite a fair value estimate around 16% — making him the best risk-adjusted bet in the next UK PM market.
why: Streeting has already declared his candidacy and doesn't need to win a by-election to compete
Frontrunner Andy Burnham faces a perilous by-election against Reform before he can even enter the leadership race
As the natural fallback if Burnham stumbles, Streeting's upside is directly tied to the most likely disruption scenario
cards.watch_item: Whether Andy Burnham secures a safe parliamentary seat — a convincing by-election win would significantly reduce Streeting's chances

cards.title: Will WHO declare Hantavirus a Public Health Emergency of International Concern this Year?
cards.headline: Hantavirus PHEIC Declaration Highly Unlikely Despite Cruise Ship Cluster
cards.summary: Despite a multi-country hantavirus cluster linked to a cruise ship generating headlines, the WHO has explicitly assessed the risk as 'low' and has not convened an Emergency Committee—making a Public Health Emergency of International Concern declaration this year very improbable.
why: WHO explicitly rates risk as 'low' with no Emergency Committee convened—a necessary precursor to any PHEIC declaration
Only ~7 confirmed cases from a single cluster, and hantavirus has very limited person-to-person transmission capability
PHEIC declarations are historically rare (7-8 in 20 years), requiring sustained international spread that hantavirus biology makes unlikely
cards.watch_item: Any evidence of sustained community transmission unlinked to the cruise ship cluster, or WHO convening an Emergency Committee, would significantly change the outlook

cards.title: Who will be Trump's next Attorney General?
cards.headline: Lee Zeldin Overpriced for Attorney General — No Evidence He's in the Running
cards.summary: Todd Blanche is currently serving as Acting AG and receiving favorable media coverage framing his tenure as an audition for the permanent role. Lee Zeldin, priced at 22.5%, has zero sourced reporting linking him to the AG position while actively leading the EPA.
why: Zeldin is running the EPA with no credible reporting of an AG move — fair value closer to 14%
Blanche is the incumbent Acting AG, running press conferences and DOJ operations daily
Major outlets are framing Blanche's stint as an 'audition,' suggesting Trump is seriously considering him
cards.watch_item: Whether Trump formally nominates Blanche before the Vacancies Act ~210-day clock forces a decision, or pivots to an entirely different candidate

cards.title: Fed decision in Oct 2027?
cards.headline: Oct 2027 Large Rate Cut Underpriced Given Recession Tail Risk
cards.summary: The market prices a >25bps Fed cut in October 2027 at just 5.5%, but over a 17-month horizon, recession risk and historical easing-cycle patterns justify roughly 13% probability — offering meaningful edge on a cheap tail-risk bet.
why: 17-month horizon creates substantial recession probability that warrants larger cuts
Contract prices across all outcomes sum to only 83.5%, structurally underpricing every YES position
Major banks still forecast rate cuts in H2 2027 despite current hawkish market pricing
cards.watch_item: Whether the Iran/Hormuz energy crisis persists or resolves — sustained high inflation would keep the Fed in hold/hike mode, eliminating cut scenarios

cards.title: 2026 2026 Midterms: Congress Balance of Power?
cards.headline: Democrats Favored to Sweep Congress in 2026 — R-House/R-Senate Looks Overpriced
cards.summary: Democrats are strongly positioned to retake the House (~85%) with the Senate a genuine toss-up leaning slightly blue (~52%), making D-House/D-Senate the most likely outcome at roughly 47% fair value versus the market's 44.5%.
why: Generic ballot shows Democrats leading by 5-10 points with presidential approval cratering
Cross-platform consensus puts Democratic House odds around 85%
Senate individual race pricing has tipped slightly toward Democrats
cards.watch_item: Republican redistricting gains in key states could net 8-13 seats, significantly reducing the Democratic House edge if maps are finalized favorably

cards.title: Seattle vs Houston
cards.headline: Seattle Underpriced Against Struggling Houston
cards.summary: Seattle is priced at 53.5% on this market but sportsbook consensus implies roughly 59-60% win probability, creating a meaningful edge on the Mariners in this matchup against a reeling Astros squad.
why: Sportsbooks have Seattle as a -155 favorite (~60%), well above the 53.5% market price here
Seattle has swept Houston 7-0 this season; Astros are 16-27 and on a 5-game losing streak
Market appears to be slow in pricing Seattle's dominance of this matchup
cards.watch_item: Bryce Miller's workload in his first start back from the injured list—a short outing could tax Seattle's bullpen and shift the game's dynamics

cards.title: Fed decision in Jul 2026?
cards.headline: July 2026 Rate Cut Looks Overpriced — Sell the Cut Contracts
cards.summary: With CPI running at 3.8% and futures markets pricing rate hikes by year-end, a July 2026 rate cut is near-impossible. The 7.5¢ price on a 25bps cut significantly overstates the true probability.
why: CPI at 3.8% makes cutting rates extremely unlikely — the inflation environment points toward holds or hikes
New Fed Chair Warsh is unlikely to make bold dovish moves in his early tenure
Fed funds futures are pricing a 51% chance of a hike by December, not cuts
cards.watch_item: A sudden financial crisis, oil price collapse, or aggressive political pressure on the new Fed Chair could revive cut expectations

cards.title: Will Donald Trump announce that the United States blockade of the Strait of Hormuz has been lifted by May 22, 2026?
cards.headline: Trump Blockade of Hormuz Unlikely to Be Lifted in Next 16 Days
cards.summary: With Trump explicitly confirming the Strait of Hormuz blockade 'remains in full force and effect' as of May 5-6 and major preconditions for a deal still unresolved, a formal lifting announcement by May 22 faces steep structural barriers despite some diplomatic momentum.
why: Trump publicly reaffirmed the blockade is active just days ago, even while touting 'Great Progress' on Iran talks
Key preconditions — especially Iran's nuclear concessions — remain far from resolution with only 16 days left
Active military hostilities in the Strait and a cancelled diplomatic trip suggest talks may be stalling
cards.watch_item: Any surprise Trump social media post or White House announcement signaling an imminent Iran deal framework — his unpredictable style means rapid reversals can't be ruled out

cards.title: New York vs Portland
cards.headline: New York vs Portland: Talent Gap Suggests Value, But Timing Risk Warrants Caution
cards.summary: The New York Liberty appear modestly underpriced at 67% against expansion team Portland, but significant uncertainty around game timing and information asymmetry makes this a no-bet situation.
why: New York is a championship-caliber team (2-0) facing a brand-new expansion team that lost its opener 98-83
Fair probability estimated around 71% for New York, creating only a thin ~4% edge over the market price
The game may have already been played, creating dangerous information asymmetry where others could know the result
cards.watch_item: Confirmation of whether this game has already been played and its final result — if the market is still open post-game, stale prices could reflect a trap rather than an opportunity

cards.title: Will Trump's approval rating increase this week?
cards.headline: Trump Approval Jump This Week Is Near-Impossible
cards.summary: Trump's approval sits at 35-38% across major polls, well below the ~40% threshold needed, with a strong downward trend and no positive catalyst in sight within the roughly 42 hours remaining.
why: Current approval (35-38%) would need a historic 2-5 point surge in under two days
Approval just hit new second-term lows on May 18-19 with a clear downward trajectory
No visible positive catalyst — structural headwinds from Iran tensions, gas prices, and base erosion persist
cards.watch_item: Any major unexpected positive event (deal, crisis resolution) in the next 42 hours that could shock polls upward

cards.title: Which country will be the next to send humans to the Moon?
cards.headline: United States Underpriced as Next to Land Humans on the Moon
cards.summary: With Artemis II successfully completed in April 2026 and two parallel lunar lander programs reducing risk, the US at 46.5% is modestly underpriced against a fair value near 50% to be the next country to put humans on the Moon.
why: Artemis II proved out the SLS/Orion system, giving real momentum toward a crewed landing by 2028-2029
NASA's dual-vendor lander strategy (SpaceX and Blue Origin) reduces single-point-of-failure risk
China's 2030 target relies entirely on unflown hardware with no backup timeline
cards.watch_item: Progress on SpaceX Starship orbital refueling demonstrations — sustained delays here would push the US landing well past 2028 and narrow the edge

cards.title: Fed decision in Jun 2026?
cards.headline: Fed Almost Certain to Hold Rates in June 2026 — Market Priced Fairly
cards.summary: With inflation running at 3.8% due to elevated energy costs from the Iran conflict and major banks pushing their first-cut forecasts to late 2026 or beyond, the Fed is overwhelmingly expected to hold rates at its June meeting. The market reflects this accurately, leaving no meaningful trading edge.
why: April CPI hit a 3-year high of 3.8%, eliminating any near-term case for easing
Goldman Sachs and UBS have pushed first-cut forecasts to December 2026 or later
The Fed has held steady at all three 2026 meetings with zero signals of a pivot
cards.watch_item: A sudden financial crisis or rapid de-escalation of the Iran conflict collapsing oil prices could shift the Fed's calculus before June 17
